questions.question: What type of plot is used to display the distribution of $24, 15, 36, 27, 18$?
The times (in minutes) for $5$ hikers to complete a trail are $23, 17, 32, 19, 28$. What is the stem for $23$ in a stem-and-leaf plot?
A stem-and-leaf plot shows fuel consumption (in miles per gallon) for $6$ vehicles. If a vehicle has a fuel consumption of $27$ miles per gallon, what is the leaf?
Which of the following distributions is symmetrical about the median?
What is the purpose of a stem-and-leaf plot?

options.B: To display the distribution of a dataset
options.C: To calculate the mean and median
options.D: To visualize the correlation between two variables

questions.question: If a stem-and-leaf plot has the stem $2$ with leaves $1, 3, 5, 7, 9$, what is the range of the data?
A wildlife researcher records the weights (in pounds) of $5$ deer: $21, 18, 25, 22, 19$. What would the stem-and-leaf plot look like?
The number of birds in $6$ different regions is $15, 28, 32, 18, 25, 20$. If these values are plotted on a stem-and-leaf plot, which region has the most birds?

Create a stem-and-leaf plot for the following distances (in miles) traveled by $8$ hikers: $12, 18, 24, 20, 15, 22, 19, 25$. Interpret the distribution and describe any patterns or trends in the data.

Suppose a park ranger collects data on the ages (in years) of $10$ animals in a wildlife sanctuary: $3, 5, 2, 8, 1, 9, 4, 7, 6, 3$. Use a stem-and-leaf plot to display the distribution of the ages and calculate the range of the data. Show your work and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand how to construct and interpret stem-and-leaf plots.
Tip 2: Provide clear instructions and examples for students to work on.
Tip 3: Encourage students to analyze and describe patterns or trends in the data.
